title: Positional Encodings — Sinusoid, RoPE, ALiBi
tagline: Attention is permutation-invariant. Three ways to break that symmetry — visualized.
summary: Transformer attention has no notion of order: 'dog bites man' and 'man bites dog' would receive identical logits. Position has to be injected from outside. Three families have won — sinusoidal (added to embeddings), RoPE (rotated queries and keys), ALiBi (a linear penalty in the attention score). Each one is shown live: heatmap, dot-product similarity matrix, extrapolation curves out to 32k tokens.
highlights: Sinusoidal heatmap with live sliders for sequence length and d_model — see the frequency bands at every dimension.
Dot-product similarity matrix between every pair of positions; watch the diagonal sharpen as |i − j| grows.
RoPE as a 2D rotation: drag a position, see q and k rotate by an angle proportional to position; the resulting dot product depends only on relative offset.
ALiBi linear-bias matrix overlaid on attention; pick a head, watch its slope decide how strongly it penalises distance.
Extrapolation panel: train at 2k tokens, test at 32k — sinusoidal collapses, RoPE drifts, ALiBi holds.
ES 667 Lec 14 demo (Transformer architecture); pairs with the attention article for the underlying QKV mechanism.
